note: Fugu County is China's magnesium capital, hosting the largest concentration of Pidgeon-process smelters.

name: Shenmu magnesium plants
note: Coal-and-dolomite belt of northern Shaanxi providing cheap energy for primary magnesium smelting.

name: Shanxi magnesium plants (Wenxi/Yuncheng)
note: Historic Pidgeon-process heartland; Shanxi remains a core province in China's dominant magnesium output.

name: Ningxia magnesium plants
note: Secondary Chinese magnesium province leveraging local coal and dolomite resources.

name: Dead Sea Magnesium
note: One of the few sizeable non-Chinese primary magnesium plants, using electrolysis from Dead Sea brine.

name: US Magnesium (Rowley)
note: Only US primary magnesium producer, extracting from Great Salt Lake brine via electrolysis.

name: Hunan bismuth refineries
note: Hunan is China's main bismuth-refining hub, recovering metal as a by-product of lead and tungsten smelting.

name: Jiangxi bismuth/tungsten plants
note: Bismuth recovered alongside Jiangxi's tungsten and polymetallic ores; part of China's ~80% supply share.

name: Guangdong bismuth (Shaoguan)
note: Lead-zinc smelting at Shaoguan yields bismuth as a refining by-product.

name: Korea Zinc Onsan (bismuth)
note: Large non-Chinese bismuth source recovered from Onsan's integrated lead-zinc refining complex.

name: Laos bismuth (Sepon area)
note: Emerging Southeast Asian bismuth output linked to lead and polymetallic processing.

name: Jacinth-Ambrosia
note: World's largest and one of the highest-grade zircon mines, the anchor of Iluka's mineral-sands output.

name: Richards Bay Minerals (zircon)
note: Major coastal dune mineral-sands operation co-producing zircon with titanium feedstock.

name: Tronox Namakwa Sands
note: West Coast mineral-sands operation producing zircon alongside titanium slag and pig iron.

name: Grande Cote Operations (GCO)
note: Large dredge-mining mineral-sands operation, one of Africa's leading zircon and ilmenite producers.

name: Moma (Kenmare)
note: Long-life titanium-zircon dredge operation, a major source of mineral-sands by-product zircon.

name: Molymet (Molibdenos y Metales)
note: World's largest rhenium producer, recovering the metal during roasting of molybdenum concentrates.

name: Sierrita rhenium plant
note: Largest US rhenium source, recovered from molybdenum at the Sierrita copper-moly mine.

name: Zhezkazgan rhenium (Kazakhmys)
note: Rhenium recovered from copper-smelting flue dust at the Zhezkazgan complex.

name: KGHM rhenium (Glogow)
note: Europe's leading rhenium producer, recovering perrhenic acid from copper-smelting gases.

name: Bagdad rhenium (Freeport)
note: Additional US rhenium recovery from molybdenum by-product at the Bagdad copper-moly operation.
